Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.
  1 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# WP-021 — COL-V3-01: Multi-user Editing Infra (yjs / CRDT)
2	
3	**Branch:** `feat/v3-col-v3-01-multiuser-yjs`
4	**Effort:** XL — 4+ weeks
5	**State:** `now`
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Required reading
10	
11	Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.
12	
13	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T3 COL-V3-01 entry (line 1268), §7 dependency table (COL-V3-01 hard-blocked by CQ-01)
14	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
15	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/hub.py` — `resume()`, ring buffer, WebSocket hub (CQ-01 shipped; read the full file)
16	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/hooks/useWorkspaceSnapshot.ts` — reconnect logic (CQ-01 shipped)
17	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — `try_commit_bundle` pipeline (the authoritative commit boundary)
18	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` — existing WebSocket route; where the new yjs Y-WebSocket endpoint will live
19	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/` — client-side session state; locate the workspace/model store
20	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py` — CMD-V3-01 commit primitive (agent and collab both flow through this)
21	
22	Already merged reference — read as patterns, do not re-apply:
23	- CQ-01 WebSocket robustness: `hub.py` resume + ring buffer; `useWorkspaceSnapshot.ts` reconnect logic
24	
25	**Hard prerequisite:** CQ-01 (WebSocket robustness) MUST be merged into `main` before this WP ships. Confirm with `git log --oneline | grep CQ-01` or search `hub.py` for the `resume()` function. Do not begin implementation until CQ-01 is present.
26	
27	---
28	
29	## Verbatim COL-V3-01 tracker entry (§6 T3, line 1268)
30	
31	**COL-V3-01 — Multi-user editing infra (yjs / CRDT)**
32	
33	_Source: R-B §6 (Motif's collab is unambiguously deeper than Rayon's) + R-A §5 (real-time multiplayer baseline). The B3 keystone — without live multi-user, every other T3 WP is reduced to single-user with a comment box._
34	
35	**Status.** `now`.
36	**Scope.** **Hard prerequisite: CQ-01 WebSocket robustness must complete before this WP ships** — unreliable WS = silent state divergence in multi-user sessions; the v3 tracker §7 calls this out explicitly. Pick **yjs** (CRDT) over WS+OT for v3 — yjs is the production-grade web CRDT, integrates with Y-WebSocket on the server, supports awareness (presence) as a side-channel, and naturally extends to offline-tolerant authoring (COL-V3-06). The v2 architecture is server-authoritative on commit with broadcast via websocket and per-user undo stacks (per §O OpenBIM Stance) — yjs slots in as the in-flight transport between commits without changing the snapshot model. Each kernel command becomes a yjs Y.Doc transaction; the server is still the authority that resolves the canonical snapshot at every commit boundary.
37	**Data model.**
38	```ts
39	// Per-model session
40	type CollabSession = {
41	  modelId: string;
42	  ydoc: Y.Doc;                       // root yjs document for this model
43	  awareness: Y.Awareness;            // presence side-channel
44	  participants: Array<{
45	    userId: string;
46	    role: 'admin' | 'editor' | 'viewer' | 'public-link-viewer';
47	    color: string;                   // assigned from --cat-* palette
48	    sessionStartedAt: number;
49	  }>;
50	};
51	
52	// In-flight kernel command (transient, pre-commit)
53	type InFlightCommand = {
54	  commandId: string;
55	  authorId: string;
56	  kind: string;                      // 'MoveWall', 'PlaceDoor', etc.
57	  ydocOps: Y.RelativePosition[];     // CRDT-encoded mutation
58	  proposedAt: number;
59	};
60	```
61	**Engine.** New server-side service `collab-orchestrator` accepts yjs Y-WebSocket connections, multiplexes by `modelId`, resolves CRDT operations, and emits `commit_bundle` events that flow through the existing `try_commit_bundle` pipeline. Conflict resolution is CRDT-native — yjs's algorithms handle two simultaneous "move wall" operations by deterministic merge; the kernel sees a single ordered command stream at the commit boundary. Per-user undo stacks remain (yjs supports `Y.UndoManager` scoped by origin). Cross-theme: T2's selection halos extend across users (each user's selection broadcasts via awareness, with their assigned color); T1's command stream is unchanged at the kernel level.
62	**UI.** No marquee surface — this WP is infrastructure. Surface tells: a top-right participant strip (initials + color, max 5 visible) appears in any view as soon as a second user joins. T2's selection halo paints at the joining user's color when they select. The `--motion-snap` token is the global in-flight motion duration (already in v2); collab-broadcast operations use the same motion arc so remote operations feel local.
63	**Acceptance.** Two browsers open the same model. User A drags a wall in 3D; User B's 3D view shows the same wall moving in 60 fps with a 2px halo at User A's color; the dim labels update on both sides. User A commits; the activity stream gains one row "User A — Move wall." Drop User A's WS connection mid-drag; on reconnect (CQ-01 robust path), state converges (yjs merges); no silent divergence. Latency budget: ≤ 100 ms perceived for in-flight ops; ≤ 250 ms for commit broadcast.
64	**Effort.** XL — 4+ weeks for one engineer plus 1 week of CQ-01 prerequisite buffering.
65	**Cross-theme references.** **Hard dep: CQ-01.** T2 (selection halos visible to others reuse the EDT-01 halo protocol with cross-user color); T9 (agent edits flow through the same yjs commit stream — agent operations look like another participant); §7 CQ-04 (Python god-file split affects the kernel's ability to ingest concurrent command streams cleanly).
66	
67	---
68	
69	## Setup
70	
71	```bash
72	# Confirm CQ-01 is merged
73	git log --oneline | grep -i "CQ-01\|websocket robustness\|hub.*resume"
74	grep -n "def resume" /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/hub.py
75	
76	git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
77	git checkout -b feat/v3-col-v3-01-multiuser-yjs
78	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
79	
80	# Install yjs server-side dependencies
81	pnpm add yjs y-websocket --filter @bim-ai/web
82	pnpm add yjs y-websocket --filter @bim-ai/core
83	
84	uv sync && pnpm install
85	```
86	
87	---
88	
89	## Implementation tasks
90	
91	### 1. Collab session types — `packages/core/src/collab.ts` (new file)
92	
93	Create a new module with the collab data types:
94	
95	```ts
96	import type * as Y from 'yjs';
97	
98	export type ParticipantRole = 'admin' | 'editor' | 'viewer' | 'public-link-viewer';
99	
100	export type Participant = {
101	  userId: string;
102	  role: ParticipantRole;
103	  color: string;               // from --cat-* CSS token palette
104	  sessionStartedAt: number;   // ms epoch
105	};
106	
107	export type CollabSession = {
108	  modelId: string;
109	  // ydoc and awareness are runtime objects — not serialised
110	  participants: Participant[];
111	};
112	
113	export type InFlightCommand = {
114	  commandId: string;
115	  authorId: string;
116	  kind: string;
117	  proposedAt: number;
118	  // ydocOps omitted from wire format; handled by yjs internally
119	};
120	
121	export type CollabAwarenessState = {
122	  userId: string;
123	  color: string;
124	  role: ParticipantRole;
125	  selectedElementIds: string[];
126	  cursorMm?: { xMm: number; yMm: number; zMm: number };
127	};
128	```
129	
130	Export all types from `packages/core/src/index.ts`.
131	
132	### 2. Server-side collab orchestrator — `app/bim_ai/collab/orchestrator.py` (new module)
133	
134	Create `app/bim_ai/collab/__init__.py` and `app/bim_ai/collab/orchestrator.py`.
135	
136	The orchestrator manages one yjs Y-WebSocket room per `modelId`:
137	
138	```python
139	from typing import Any
140	import asyncio
141	import logging
142	from collections import defaultdict
143	
144	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
145	
146	
147	class CollabRoom:
148	    """One room = one model's in-flight CRDT state."""
149	
150	    def __init__(self, model_id: str) -> None:
151	        self.model_id = model_id
152	        self.connections: set[Any] = set()  # WebSocket connections
153	        self.awareness_states: dict[str, dict] = {}  # userId → awareness state
154	
155	    async def broadcast(self, message: bytes, exclude: Any = None) -> None:
156	        """Broadcast a yjs sync message to all connections except sender."""
157	        dead = set()
158	        for conn in self.connections:
159	            if conn is exclude:
160	                continue
161	            try:
162	                await conn.send_bytes(message)
163	            except Exception:
164	                dead.add(conn)
165	        self.connections -= dead
166	
167	    def join(self, conn: Any) -> None:
168	        self.connections.add(conn)
169	
170	    def leave(self, conn: Any) -> None:
171	        self.connections.discard(conn)
172	
173	
174	class CollabOrchestrator:
175	    """Multiplexes collab rooms by model_id."""
176	
177	    def __init__(self) -> None:
178	        self._rooms: dict[str, CollabRoom] = defaultdict(
179	            lambda: CollabRoom("")  # replaced on first access
180	        )
181	
182	    def get_room(self, model_id: str) -> CollabRoom:
183	        if model_id not in self._rooms:
184	            self._rooms[model_id] = CollabRoom(model_id)
185	        return self._rooms[model_id]
186	
187	    def remove_empty_rooms(self) -> None:
188	        empty = [mid for mid, r in self._rooms.items() if not r.connections]
189	        for mid in empty:
190	            del self._rooms[mid]
191	
192	
193	# Module-level singleton
194	_orchestrator: CollabOrchestrator | None = None
195	
196	
197	def get_orchestrator() -> CollabOrchestrator:
198	    global _orchestrator
199	    if _orchestrator is None:
200	        _orchestrator = CollabOrchestrator()
201	    return _orchestrator
202	```
203	
204	### 3. Y-WebSocket route — `app/bim_ai/routes_api.py`
205	
206	Add a new WebSocket endpoint that speaks the yjs Y-WebSocket protocol:
207	
208	```python
209	@api_router.websocket("/models/{model_id}/collab")
210	async def collab_ws(
211	    websocket: WebSocket,
212	    model_id: UUID,
213	    orchestrator: CollabOrchestrator = Depends(get_orchestrator),
214	) -> None:
215	    """yjs Y-WebSocket endpoint for real-time collaboration on a model."""
216	    await websocket.accept()
217	    room = orchestrator.get_room(str(model_id))
218	    room.join(websocket)
219	    try:
220	        while True:
221	            data = await websocket.receive_bytes()
222	            # Relay yjs sync/awareness messages to all other participants
223	            await room.broadcast(data, exclude=websocket)
224	    except WebSocketDisconnect:
225	        room.leave(websocket)
226	        orchestrator.remove_empty_rooms()
227	        logger.info("collab ws disconnect model=%s", model_id)
228	```
229	
230	### 4. Participant strip component — `packages/web/src/collab/ParticipantStrip.tsx` (new file)
231	
232	Create a React component that renders the top-right participant strip:
233	
234	- Displays up to 5 participant avatars (initials + color ring from `--cat-*` palette).
235	- When > 5 participants, shows "+N" overflow chip.
236	- Each avatar has a tooltip with the participant's name + role.
237	- Uses only CSS token variables — no hex literals.
238	- Position: absolute top-right inside the canvas container; `z-index: var(--z-collab-strip)` (add this token to the design system if it does not exist).
239	
240	```tsx
241	import type { Participant } from '@bim-ai/core';
242	
243	interface Props {
244	  participants: Participant[];
245	  currentUserId: string;
246	}
247	
248	export function ParticipantStrip({ participants, currentUserId }: Props) {
249	  // ... render up to 5 avatars + overflow
250	}
251	```
252	
253	### 5. Client-side collab provider — `packages/web/src/collab/CollabProvider.tsx` (new file)
254	
255	Create a React context + provider that:
256	1. Connects to `ws://<host>/api/models/<modelId>/collab` via `y-websocket`.
257	2. Stores the yjs `Y.Doc` and `Y.Awareness` in context.
258	3. Exposes `participants` (derived from awareness states), `localUser`, and `broadcastSelection` (updates awareness with selected element ids).
259	4. Reconnects automatically using the CQ-01 ring-buffer reconnect pattern from `useWorkspaceSnapshot.ts`.
260	
261	```tsx
262	import * as Y from 'yjs';
263	import { WebsocketProvider } from 'y-websocket';
264	import type { CollabAwarenessState, Participant } from '@bim-ai/core';
265	
266	export const CollabContext = React.createContext<CollabContextValue | null>(null);
267	
268	export function CollabProvider({ modelId, children }: { modelId: string; children: React.ReactNode }) {
269	  // ... setup ydoc, awareness, websocket provider
270	  // ... derive participants from awareness states
271	  // ... expose broadcastSelection
272	}
273	
274	export function useCollab(): CollabContextValue {
275	  const ctx = React.useContext(CollabContext);
276	  if (!ctx) throw new Error('useCollab must be used within CollabProvider');
277	  return ctx;
278	}
279	```
280	
281	### 6. Selection halo extension for remote users
282	
283	Locate the existing selection halo rendering code (search `packages/web/src/` for `halo`, `selectionHalo`, or the equivalent). Extend it to accept a `remoteSelections: Array<{ elementId: string; color: string }>` prop and render halos for remote participants using their assigned color from `--cat-*` palette. The halo width must use the existing `--draft-cut-major` or equivalent selection token.
284	
285	### 7. `commit_bundle` event → `try_commit_bundle` bridge
286	
287	When the orchestrator receives a yjs transaction that carries a `commit_bundle` event (a special yjs message type defined in step 4), it must call `apply_bundle` via the existing CMD-V3-01 REST route or an internal function call. For v3.0, the simplest approach is:
288	
289	- The client emits a standard HTTP `POST /api/models/{model_id}/bundles` when the user chooses to commit.
290	- The yjs in-flight CRDT layer is used only for pre-commit, live-preview mutations.
291	- The kernel commit boundary remains `try_commit_bundle` — yjs does not replace it.
292	
293	Document this in a `# Architecture note` comment inside `orchestrator.py`.
294	
295	### 8. Tests
296	
297	- `app/tests/collab/test_collab_orchestrator.py`:
298	  - `test_room_created_on_first_access` — `get_room` creates a `CollabRoom`.
299	  - `test_broadcast_reaches_all_connections` — mock two connections; broadcast reaches both.
300	  - `test_broadcast_excludes_sender` — sender is excluded.
301	  - `test_leave_removes_connection` — connection removed after leave.
302	  - `test_remove_empty_rooms` — empty rooms pruned.
303	- `app/tests/api/test_collab_ws.py`:
304	  - `test_collab_ws_accepts_connection` — FastAPI TestClient WS test; connection accepted.
305	  - `test_collab_ws_relays_bytes` — bytes sent by one client are received by another.
306	  - `test_collab_ws_disconnect_cleans_up` — room is pruned after disconnect.
307	
308	---
309	
310	## Implementation rules
311	
312	- **CQ-01 is a hard prerequisite.** Do not implement this WP until `hub.py` contains the `resume()` function (CQ-01 shipped). The CI gate in `app/tests/test_hub.py` must pass before branching.
313	- **No AI in any binary.** yjs CRDT is deterministic. The collab orchestrator dispatches to `try_commit_bundle` at the commit boundary — same deterministic pipeline as the REST API.
314	- **API-V3-01 contract.** The new `/api/models/{model_id}/collab` WebSocket endpoint should be documented in `api/registry.py` under a new `collab` tool descriptor (or at minimum as a comment; check the registry pattern and follow it).
315	- **No hex literals in chrome.** All React components in this WP (`ParticipantStrip.tsx`, `CollabProvider.tsx`) must use only CSS token variables.
316	- **yjs is the in-flight transport, not the commit store.** The kernel snapshot model is unchanged. yjs state is ephemeral between commits. The server is still authoritative at the commit boundary.
317	- **Pydantic alias discipline** does not apply to Python collab types (they are not Pydantic models), but follow the same `snake_case` Python / `camelCase` wire convention.
318	- **No changes to `engine.py`.** The commit pipeline is unchanged. CQ-04 (engine.py split) is a separate WP.
319	
320	---
321

343	- Wall drag in tab A appears in tab B within 100 ms.

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import time
4	from collections import deque
5	from copy import deepcopy
6	from typing import Any
7	
8	from fastapi import WebSocket
9	
10	BACKPRESSURE_THRESHOLD = 8
11	
12	
13	class Hub:
14	    """WebSocket subscribers with delta broadcasts and ephemeral presence snapshots."""
15	
16	    def __init__(self) -> None:
17	        self._rooms: dict[str, set[WebSocket]] = {}
18	        self._presence: dict[str, dict[str, dict[str, Any]]] = {}
19	        # WebSocket python id → (model_id, peer_id)
20	        self._socket_meta: dict[int, tuple[str, str]] = {}
21	        # Per-model monotonic sequence counter
22	        self._seq: dict[str, int] = {}
23	        # Per-model ring buffer: deque of (seq, ts, payload_copy)
24	        self._buffer: dict[str, deque[tuple[int, float, dict[str, Any]]]] = {}
25	        # Per-socket in-flight send depth (keyed on id(websocket))
26	        self._send_queue_depth: dict[int, int] = {}
27	
28	    def subscribe(self, model_id: str, websocket: WebSocket) -> None:
29	        wsid = id(websocket)
30	        _, pid = self._socket_meta.get(wsid, (model_id, ""))
31	        self._socket_meta[wsid] = (model_id, pid)
32	        self._rooms.setdefault(model_id, set()).add(websocket)
33	
34	    def unregister(self, websocket: WebSocket) -> None:
35	        wsid = id(websocket)
36	        self._send_queue_depth.pop(wsid, None)
37	        tup = self._socket_meta.pop(wsid, None)
38	        if not tup:
39	            return
40	        mid, peer_id = tup
41	        if peer_id:
42	            peers = self._presence.get(mid)
43	            if peers:
44	                peers.pop(peer_id, None)
45	
46	        room = self._rooms.get(mid)
47	        if room:
48	            room.discard(websocket)
49	            if not room:
50	                self._rooms.pop(mid, None)
51	
52	    def set_peer_id(self, websocket: WebSocket, peer_id: str) -> tuple[str | None, str]:
53	        wsid = id(websocket)
54	        tup = self._socket_meta.get(wsid)
55	        if tup is None:
56	            return None, peer_id
57	        mid, _ = tup
58	        self._socket_meta[wsid] = (mid, peer_id)
59	        return mid, peer_id
60	
61	    def touch_presence(
62	        self, model_id: str, peer_id: str, patch: dict[str, Any]
63	    ) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
64	        room = self._presence.setdefault(model_id, {})
65	
66	        base = dict(room.get(peer_id, {}))
67	
68	        base.update(patch)
69	
70	        base.setdefault("peerId", peer_id)
71	
72	        room[peer_id] = base
73	
74	        return room
75	
76	    async def publish(self, model_id: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> int:
77	        """Stamp payload with next seq, append to ring buffer, and broadcast."""
78	        seq = self._seq.get(model_id, 0) + 1
79	        self._seq[model_id] = seq
80	        stamped = {**payload, "seq": seq}
81	
82	        buf = self._buffer.setdefault(model_id, deque(maxlen=512))
83	        buf.append((seq, time.monotonic(), deepcopy(stamped)))
84	
85	        await self.broadcast_json(model_id, stamped)
86	        return seq
87	
88	    def resume(self, model_id: str, from_seq: int) -> list[dict[str, Any]] | None:
89	        """Return buffered payloads with seq > from_seq, or None on window miss."""
90	        buf = self._buffer.get(model_id)
91	        if not buf:
92	            return None
93	
94	        oldest_seq = buf[0][0]
95	        if from_seq + 1 < oldest_seq:
96	            return None
97	
98	        return [payload for seq, _ts, payload in buf if seq > from_seq]
99	
100	    async def broadcast_presence(self, model_id: str) -> None:
101	        peers = dict(self._presence.get(model_id, {}))
102	        payload: dict[str, Any] = {
103	            "type": "presence_state",
104	            "modelId": model_id,
105	            "payload": {"peers": peers},
106	        }
107	        await self.broadcast_json(model_id, payload)
108	
109	    async def broadcast_json(self, model_id: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
110	        room = self._rooms.get(model_id)
111	        if not room:
112	            return
113	
114	        stale: list[WebSocket] = []
115	
116	        for ws in list(room):
117	            wsid = id(ws)
118	            depth = self._send_queue_depth.get(wsid, 0)
119	            if depth >= BACKPRESSURE_THRESHOLD:
120	                try:
121	                    await ws.close(code=1011)
122	                except Exception:
123	                    pass
124	                stale.append(ws)
125	                continue
126	            self._send_queue_depth[wsid] = depth + 1
127	            try:
128	                await ws.send_json(payload)
129	            except Exception:
130	                stale.append(ws)
131	            finally:
132	                self._send_queue_depth[wsid] = max(
133	                    0, self._send_queue_depth.get(wsid, 1) - 1
134	                )
135	
136	        for ws in stale:
137	            self.unregister(ws)
138

165	class CreateEmptyModelBody(BaseModel):
166	    slug: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=128)
167	    # VIE-06: optional template id (e.g. "residential-eu") to seed the model.
168	    template_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="templateId")

171	@api_router.post("/projects/{project_id}/models")
172	async def create_empty_model(
173	    project_id: UUID,
174	    body: CreateEmptyModelBody,
175	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
176	) -> dict[str, Any]:
177	    proj = await session.get(ProjectRecord, project_id)
178	    if proj is None:
179	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Project not found")
180	    mid = uuid4()
181	
182	    if body.template_id:
183	        if not template_exists(body.template_id):
184	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Template '{body.template_id}' not found")
185	        try:
186	            seed_doc = load_template_snapshot(body.template_id)
187	        except (FileNotFoundError, LookupError) as exc:
188	            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(exc)) from exc
189	        # Reset revision so the new model starts at 1 regardless of template state.
190	        seed_doc.revision = 1
191	    else:
192	        seed_doc = Document(revision=1, elements={})  # type: ignore[arg-type]
193	
194	    # KRN-06: every new model has the singleton internal_origin from inception.
195	    ensure_internal_origin(seed_doc)
196	    ensure_sun_settings(seed_doc)
197	    wire = document_to_wire(seed_doc)
198	    row = ModelRecord(
199	        id=mid,
200	        project_id=project_id,
201	        slug=body.slug,
202	        revision=seed_doc.revision,
203	        document=wire,
204	    )
205	    session.add(row)
206	    try:
207	        await session.commit()
208	    except IntegrityError:
209	        await session.rollback()
210	        raise HTTPException(
211	            status_code=409,
212	            detail="Model slug already exists for this project",
213	        ) from None
214	    return {
215	        "id": str(mid),
216	        "projectId": str(project_id),
217	        "slug": body.slug,
218	        "revision": row.revision,
219	        "templateId": body.template_id,
220	    }

245	@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/snapshot")
246	async def snapshot(
247	    model_id: UUID,
248	    expandLinks: bool = False,  # noqa: N803 — wire-format alias
249	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
250	) -> dict[str, Any]:
251	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
252	    if row is None:
253	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
254	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
255	    # KRN-06: backfill internal_origin for legacy models that pre-date this WP.
256	    # Read-only — we don't persist; the next command commit will pick it up.
257	    ensure_internal_origin(doc)
258	    ensure_sun_settings(doc)
259	    elements_wire = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc.elements.items()}
260	    if expandLinks:
261	        # FED-01: inline every linked source's elements with provenance markers
262	        # so renderers can ghost them. Default snapshot omits these to keep the
263	        # payload small.
264	        elements_wire = await _expand_host_links(session, doc, elements_wire)
265	    link_source_revisions = await _resolve_link_source_revisions(session, doc)
266	    out: dict[str, Any] = {
267	        "modelId": str(row.id),
268	        "revision": doc.revision,
269	        "elements": elements_wire,
270	        "violations": violations_wire(doc.elements),
271	    }
272	    if link_source_revisions:
273	        # FED-01 polish: per-source current revisions so the UI can render
274	        # drift badges on pinned links without an extra round-trip.
275	        out["linkSourceRevisions"] = link_source_revisions
276	    return out

279	async def _resolve_link_source_revisions(
280	    session: AsyncSession, host_doc: Document
281	) -> dict[str, int]:
282	    """Look up the current revision of every distinct source UUID referenced
283	    by a ``link_model`` element in ``host_doc``. Missing sources are omitted
284	    from the result. Used by the FED-01 drift-badge UI."""
285	
286	    out: dict[str, int] = {}
287	    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
288	        if not isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
289	            continue
290	        src_uuid_str = elem.source_model_id
291	        if src_uuid_str in out:
292	            continue
293	        try:
294	            src_uuid = UUID(src_uuid_str)
295	        except ValueError:
296	            continue
297	        src_row = await load_model_row(session, src_uuid)
298	        if src_row is None:
299	            continue
300	        try:
301	            src_doc = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
302	        except Exception:
303	            continue
304	        out[src_uuid_str] = int(src_doc.revision)
305	    return out
306	
307	
308	async def _expand_host_links(
309	    session: AsyncSession,
310	    host_doc: Document,
311	    host_elements_wire: dict[str, Any],
312	) -> dict[str, Any]:
313	    """FED-01: resolve every ``link_model`` row's source document from DB and
314	    pass it through ``expand_links`` to inline transformed source elements.
315	
316	    Sources are loaded at their pinned revision when set (replayed via the
317	    undo stack), or at their current revision otherwise. Missing sources are
318	    skipped silently — the host is still authoritative.
319	    """
320	
321	    cache: dict[tuple[str, int | None], Document | None] = {}
322	
323	    async def _load_source(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
324	        cache_key = (source_uuid_str, source_rev)
325	        if cache_key in cache:
326	            return cache[cache_key]
327	        try:
328	            source_uuid = UUID(source_uuid_str)
329	        except ValueError:
330	            cache[cache_key] = None
331	            return None
332	        src_row = await load_model_row(session, source_uuid)
333	        if src_row is None:
334	            cache[cache_key] = None
335	            return None
336	        current = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
337	        if source_rev is None or source_rev == current.revision:
338	            cache[cache_key] = current
339	            return current
340	        # Replay backwards through the undo stack to land at the requested
341	        # revision (mirrors the diff endpoint's logic).
342	        try:
343	            doc_at = await _document_at_revision(session, source_uuid, current, source_rev)
344	        except HTTPException:
345	            cache[cache_key] = None
346	            return None
347	        cache[cache_key] = doc_at
348	        return doc_at
349	
350	    # Pre-load every link's source synchronously (the providers callable in
351	    # ``expand_links`` is sync; we resolve up-front).
352	    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
353	        if isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
354	            await _load_source(elem.source_model_id, elem.source_model_revision)
355	
356	    def _provider(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
357	        return cache.get((source_uuid_str, source_rev))
358	
359	    return expand_links(host_doc, host_elements_wire, _provider)
360	
361	
362	async def _document_at_revision(
363	    session: AsyncSession, model_id: UUID, current: Document, target_rev: int
364	) -> Document:
365	    """Reconstruct ``current`` rolled back to ``target_rev`` by replaying
366	    undo bundles in reverse-revision order. Returns a fresh ``Document``;
367	    the ``revision`` attribute is informational and may not equal
368	    ``target_rev`` after the engine bumps the counter — element state is
369	    what matters for diff.
370	    """
371	    if target_rev == current.revision:
372	        return clone_document(current)
373	    res = await session.execute(
374	        select(UndoStackRecord)
375	        .where(
376	            UndoStackRecord.model_id == model_id,
377	            UndoStackRecord.revision_after > target_rev,
378	            UndoStackRecord.revision_after <= current.revision,
379	        )
380	        .order_by(desc(UndoStackRecord.revision_after), desc(UndoStackRecord.id))
381	    )
382	    rolling = clone_document(current)
383	    for entry in res.scalars().all():
384	        ok, new_doc, _cmds, _viols, _code = try_commit_bundle(rolling, list(entry.undo_commands))
385	        if ok and new_doc is not None:
386	            rolling = new_doc
387	        else:
388	            raise HTTPException(
389	                status_code=409,
390	                detail=(
391	                    "Cannot reconstruct historical revision — undo replay failed at "
392	                    f"revision_after={entry.revision_after}"
393	                ),
394	            )
395	    return rolling
396	
397	
398	@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/diff")
399	async def model_diff(
400	    model_id: UUID,
401	    fromRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803 — wire-format alias
402	    toRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803
403	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
404	) -> dict[str, Any]:
405	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
406	    if row is None:
407	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
408	    current = Document.model_validate(row.document)
409	
410	    to_rev = toRev if toRev is not None else current.revision
411	    from_rev = fromRev if fromRev is not None else max(1, to_rev - 1)
412	
413	    if from_rev > current.revision or to_rev > current.revision:
414	        raise HTTPException(
415	            status_code=400,
416	            detail=(
417	                f"Revision out of range: model is at revision {current.revision} "
418	                f"(fromRev={from_rev}, toRev={to_rev})."
419	            ),
420	        )
421	
422	    doc_to = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, to_rev)
423	    doc_from = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, from_rev)
424	
425	    elements_from = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_from.elements.items()}
426	    elements_to = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_to.elements.items()}
427	
428	    diff = compute_element_diff(elements_from, elements_to)
429	    return {
430	        "modelId": str(model_id),
431	        "fromRevision": from_rev,
432	        "toRevision": to_rev,
433	        **diff,
434	    }

459	    if row is None:
460	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
461	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
462	    viols = violations_wire(doc.elements)
463	    err_ct = sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("severity") == "error")
464	    block_ct = sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("blocking") is True)
465	    return {
466	        "modelId": str(model_id),
467	        "revision": doc.revision,
468	        "violations": viols,
469	        "summary": compute_model_summary(doc),
470	        "checks": {"errorViolationCount": err_ct, "blockingViolationCount": block_ct},
471	    }

790	    "/models/{model_id}/schedules/{schedule_id}/table",

792	)

794	    model_id: UUID,
795	    schedule_id: str,
796	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
797	    fmt: Annotated[str, Query(alias="format")] = "json",
798	    columns: Annotated[str | None, Query(alias="columns")] = None,
799	    include_schedule_totals_csv: Annotated[bool, Query(alias="includeScheduleTotalsCsv")] = False,
800	) -> dict[str, Any] | PlainTextResponse:

804	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
805	    try:
806	        payload = derive_schedule_table(doc, schedule_id)
807	    except ValueError as exc:
808	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from exc
809	    if fmt.strip().lower() == "csv":
810	        export_payload = payload
811	        if columns and columns.strip():
812	            wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
813	            if wanted:
814	                export_payload = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
815	        csv_body = schedule_payload_to_csv(
816	            export_payload,
817	            include_totals_csv=include_schedule_totals_csv,
818	        )
819	        safe = "".join(ch for ch in schedule_id if ch.isalnum() or ch in ("-", "_")) or "schedule"
820	        return PlainTextResponse(
821	            csv_body,
822	            media_type="text/csv; charset=utf-8",
823	            headers={"Content-Disposition": f'attachment; filename="{safe}.csv"'},
824	        )
825	    out = payload
826	    if columns and columns.strip():
827	        wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
828	        if wanted:
829	            out = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
830	    return out

854	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
855	# FED-04 — IFC → shadow-model link import
856	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
857	
858	
859	class ImportIfcBody(BaseModel):
860	    """FED-04: payload for ``POST /api/models/{host_id}/import-ifc``.
861	
862	    Either ``file_text`` (inline IFC STEP) or ``file_path`` (server-side path
863	    readable by the FastAPI process) must be supplied. ``slug`` names the new
864	    shadow-model row; ``link_name`` is the host-side display name for the
865	    auto-created ``link_model`` element. Both have sensible defaults so a
866	    minimal request just sends the IFC bytes.
867	    """
868	
869	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
870	    file_text: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="fileText")
871	    file_path: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="filePath")
872	    slug: str = Field(default="ifc-import", min_length=1, max_length=128)
873	    link_name: str = Field(default="Linked IFC", alias="linkName")
874	
875	
876	@api_router.post("/models/{host_id}/import-ifc")
877	async def import_ifc_to_shadow_link(
878	    host_id: UUID,
879	    body: ImportIfcBody,
880	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
881	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
882	) -> dict[str, Any]:
883	    """FED-04: import an IFC file as a brand-new shadow bim-ai model + auto-
884	    create a ``link_model`` row in the host pointing at it.
885	
886	    Round-trip: parse IFC → ``authoritativeReplay_v0`` command bundle →
887	    apply to a fresh ``ModelRecord`` in the same project → run
888	    ``createLinkModel`` against the host. The shadow model is independent
889	    from then on (host edits never reach back into it; the host treats its
890	    elements as read-only renderable context per FED-01).
891	    """
892	
893	    from bim_ai.export_ifc import build_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_sketch_v0
894	    from bim_ai.engine import (
895	        try_apply_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_v0,
896	        try_commit,
897	    )
898	
899	    # Resolve host first so we can mirror its project_id onto the shadow.
900	    host_row = await load_model_row(session, host_id)
901	    if host_row is None:
902	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Host model not found")
903	
904	    # Read the IFC text. The endpoint accepts either inline text or a path.
905	    if body.file_text is not None:
906	        step_text = body.file_text
907	    elif body.file_path is not None:
908	        try:
909	            with open(body.file_path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
910	                step_text = fh.read()
911	        except OSError as exc:
912	            raise HTTPException(
913	                status_code=400, detail=f"Cannot read IFC file: {exc}"
914	            ) from exc
915	    else:
916	        raise HTTPException(
917	            status_code=400,
918	            detail="import-ifc requires either fileText or filePath in the request body",
919	        )
920	
921	    sketch = build_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_sketch_v0(step_text)
922	    if sketch.get("available") is not True:
923	        raise HTTPException(
924	            status_code=400,
925	            detail={
926	                "reason": "ifc_replay_unavailable",
927	                "ifcReason": sketch.get("reason"),
928	            },
929	        )
930	
931	    # 1. Create the shadow model row in the host's project.
932	    shadow_id = uuid4()
933	    shadow_doc: Document = Document(revision=1, elements={})  # type: ignore[arg-type]
934	    ensure_internal_origin(shadow_doc)
935	    ensure_sun_settings(shadow_doc)
936	
937	    # 2. Apply the replay bundle in-memory.
938	    ok, replayed_doc, applied_cmds, _viols, code = try_apply_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_v0(
939	        shadow_doc, sketch
940	    )
941	    if not ok or replayed_doc is None:
942	        raise HTTPException(
943	            status_code=400,
944	            detail={"reason": "ifc_replay_failed", "code": code},
945	        )
946	
947	    # 3. Persist the shadow model.
948	    shadow_row = ModelRecord(
949	        id=shadow_id,
950	        project_id=host_row.project_id,
951	        slug=body.slug,
952	        revision=replayed_doc.revision,
953	        document=document_to_wire(replayed_doc),
954	    )
955	    session.add(shadow_row)
956	    try:
957	        await session.flush()
958	    except IntegrityError:
959	        await session.rollback()
960	        raise HTTPException(
961	            status_code=409,
962	            detail="Shadow model slug already exists for this project — pass a unique 'slug'",
963	        ) from None
964	
965	    # 4. Build a createLinkModel command and apply it to the host.
966	    suggested_position = {"xMm": 0.0, "yMm": 0.0, "zMm": 0.0}
967	    host_doc = Document.model_validate(host_row.document)
968	    create_link = {
969	        "type": "createLinkModel",
970	        "name": body.link_name,
971	        "sourceModelId": str(shadow_id),
972	        "positionMm": suggested_position,
973	        "rotationDeg": 0.0,
974	        "originAlignmentMode": "origin_to_origin",
975	    }
976	    try:
977	        host_ok, new_host_doc, _cmd, host_viols, host_code = try_commit(host_doc, create_link)
978	    except Exception as exc:
979	        await session.rollback()
980	        raise HTTPException(
981	            status_code=400, detail=f"createLinkModel failed: {exc}"
982	        ) from exc
983	    if not host_ok or new_host_doc is None:
984	        await session.rollback()
985	        raise HTTPException(
986	            status_code=409,
987	            detail={
988	                "reason": host_code,
989	                "violations": [v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for v in host_viols],
990	            },
991	        )
992	
993	    # The new link_model element id is the only one missing from doc_before.
994	    new_link_ids = set(new_host_doc.elements.keys()) - set(host_doc.elements.keys())
995	    if len(new_link_ids) != 1:
996	        await session.rollback()
997	        raise HTTPException(
998	            status_code=500,
999	            detail="Internal: createLinkModel did not produce exactly one new element",
1000	        )
1001	    link_element_id = next(iter(new_link_ids))
1002	
1003	    # Persist the host. Keep the undo-stack record so the import is undoable.
1004	    host_row.document = document_to_wire(new_host_doc)  # type: ignore[assignment]
1005	    host_row.revision = new_host_doc.revision
1006	    await session.commit()
1007	
1008	    # Broadcast the host's delta so connected clients pick up the link.
1009	    try:
1010	        await hub.publish(
1011	            host_id,
1012	            {
1013	                "type": "delta",
1014	                "modelId": str(host_id),
1015	                "revision": new_host_doc.revision,
1016	            },
1017	        )
1018	    except Exception:
1019	        # Hub failures must not roll back the import.
1020	        pass
1021	
1022	    return {
1023	        "linkedModelId": str(shadow_id),
1024	        "linkElementId": link_element_id,
1025	        "suggestedLinkPosition": suggested_position,
1026	        "appliedReplayCommandCount": len(applied_cmds),
1027	        "shadowModelSlug": body.slug,
1028	    }

1036	class ImportDxfBody(BaseModel):
1037	    """FED-04: payload for ``POST /api/models/{host_id}/import-dxf``.
1038	
1039	    Either ``file_path`` (server-side path readable by the FastAPI process)
1040	    must be supplied. ``level_id`` names the host level the underlay is
1041	    attached to. ``origin_mm`` / ``rotation_deg`` / ``scale_factor`` let the
1042	    caller place the linework; defaults centre on the project origin with
1043	    no rotation.
1044	    """
1045	
1046	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
1047	    file_path: str = Field(alias="filePath")
1048	    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
1049	    name: str = Field(default="DXF Underlay")
1050	    origin_mm: dict[str, float] | None = Field(default=None, alias="originMm")
1051	    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="rotationDeg")
1052	    scale_factor: float = Field(default=1.0, alias="scaleFactor", gt=0)
1053	
1054	
1055	@api_router.post("/models/{host_id}/import-dxf")
1056	async def import_dxf(
1057	    host_id: UUID,
1058	    body: ImportDxfBody,
1059	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
1060	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
1061	) -> dict[str, Any]:
1062	    """FED-04: parse a DXF file and materialise a ``link_dxf`` element.
1063	
1064	    The route reads the file at ``body.file_path``, runs the ``ezdxf``
1065	    parser, then dispatches a single ``createLinkDxf`` engine command on
1066	    the host. Returns the new ``link_dxf`` element id so the frontend can
1067	    open ManageLinksDialog with the new entry highlighted.
1068	    """
1069	
1070	    from pathlib import Path as _Path
1071	
1072	    from bim_ai.dxf_import import parse_dxf_to_linework
1073	
1074	    host_row = await load_model_row(session, host_id)
1075	    if host_row is None:
1076	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Host model not found")
1077	
1078	    dxf_path = _Path(body.file_path)
1079	    if not dxf_path.is_file():
1080	        raise HTTPException(
1081	            status_code=400, detail=f"DXF file not found at filePath: {body.file_path}"
1082	        )
1083	
1084	    try:
1085	        linework = parse_dxf_to_linework(dxf_path)
1086	    except Exception as exc:
1087	        raise HTTPException(
1088	            status_code=400, detail=f"DXF parse failed: {exc}"
1089	        ) from exc
1090	
1091	    host_doc = Document.model_validate(host_row.document)
1092	    if body.level_id not in host_doc.elements or not isinstance(
1093	        host_doc.elements[body.level_id], LevelElem
1094	    ):
1095	        raise HTTPException(
1096	            status_code=400, detail="levelId must reference an existing Level on the host model"
1097	        )
1098	
1099	    create_cmd = {
1100	        "type": "createLinkDxf",
1101	        "name": body.name,
1102	        "levelId": body.level_id,
1103	        "originMm": body.origin_mm or {"xMm": 0.0, "yMm": 0.0},
1104	        "rotationDeg": float(body.rotation_deg),
1105	        "scaleFactor": float(body.scale_factor),
1106	        "linework": linework,
1107	    }
1108	    try:
1109	        ok, new_doc, _cmds, viols, code = try_commit_bundle(host_doc, [create_cmd])
1110	    except Exception as exc:
1111	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"createLinkDxf failed: {exc}") from exc
1112	    if not ok or new_doc is None:
1113	        raise HTTPException(
1114	            status_code=409,
1115	            detail={
1116	                "reason": code,
1117	                "violations": [v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for v in viols],
1118	            },
1119	        )
1120	
1121	    new_link_dxf_ids = [
1122	        eid
1123	        for eid in set(new_doc.elements.keys()) - set(host_doc.elements.keys())
1124	        if getattr(new_doc.elements[eid], "kind", None) == "link_dxf"
1125	    ]
1126	    if len(new_link_dxf_ids) != 1:
1127	        raise HTTPException(
1128	            status_code=500,
1129	            detail="Internal: createLinkDxf did not produce exactly one new link_dxf element",
1130	        )
1131	    link_element_id = new_link_dxf_ids[0]
1132	
1133	    host_row.document = document_to_wire(new_doc)  # type: ignore[assignment]
1134	    host_row.revision = new_doc.revision
1135	    await session.commit()
1136	
1137	    try:
1138	        await hub.publish(
1139	            host_id,
1140	            {
1141	                "type": "delta",
1142	                "modelId": str(host_id),
1143	                "revision": new_doc.revision,
1144	            },
1145	        )
1146	    except Exception:
1147	        pass
1148	
1149	    return {
1150	        "linkedElementId": link_element_id,
1151	        "lineworkCount": len(linework),
1152	    }

1183	
1184	class CommandBundleRequest(BaseModel):
1185	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)
1186	
1187	    bundle: CommandBundle
1188	    mode: str = Field(default="dry_run")
1189	    user_id: str | None = Field(default="local-dev", alias="userId")
1190	
1191	
1192	@api_router.post("/models/{model_id}/bundles")
1193	async def apply_bundle_route(
1194	    model_id: UUID,
1195	    body: CommandBundleRequest,
1196	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
1197	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
1198	) -> dict[str, Any]:
1199	    """CMD-V3-01: submit a CommandBundle; returns BundleResult.
1200	
1201	    mode='dry_run' (default) — validates without mutating.
1202	    mode='commit'            — commits if no blocking advisories fire.
1203	    HTTP 409 on revision_conflict or assumption_log_required / malformed.
1204	    """
1205	    from datetime import UTC, datetime
1206	
1207	    from bim_ai.engine import compute_delta_wire, diff_undo_cmds
1208	    from bim_ai.routes_deps import delete_redos, document_to_wire
1209	    from bim_ai.tables import UndoStackRecord
1210	
1211	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
1212	    if row is None:
1213	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
1214	
1215	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
1216	    mode = body.mode if body.mode in ("dry_run", "commit") else "dry_run"
1217	
1218	    result = _apply_bundle(doc, body.bundle, mode)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
1219	
1220	    # Surface blocking advisory classes as HTTP 409
1221	    _BLOCKING_ADVISORY_CLASSES = {
1222	        "revision_conflict",
1223	        "assumption_log_required",
1224	        "assumption_log_malformed",
1225	        "assumption_log_duplicate_key",
1226	        "direct_main_commit_forbidden",
1227	        "option_routing_not_yet_implemented",
1228	    }
1229	    if not result.applied and result.violations:
1230	        blocking_classes = {v.get("advisoryClass") for v in result.violations}
1231	        if blocking_classes & _BLOCKING_ADVISORY_CLASSES:
1232	            raise HTTPException(
1233	                status_code=409,
1234	                detail={
1235	                    "result": result.model_dump(by_alias=True),
1236	                    "violations": result.violations,
1237	                },
1238	            )
1239	
1240	    if result.applied and result.new_revision is not None:
1241	        # Re-run engine to get new_doc for persisting (deterministic — same result)
1242	        ok, new_doc, _cmds, _viols, _code = try_commit_bundle(doc, body.bundle.commands)
1243	        if ok and new_doc is not None:
1244	            uid = body.user_id or "local-dev"
1245	            doc_before = clone_document(doc)
1246	            undo_cmds = diff_undo_cmds(doc_before, new_doc)
1247	            await delete_redos(session, model_id, uid)
1248	
1249	            session.add(
1250	                UndoStackRecord(
1251	                    model_id=model_id,
1252	                    user_id=uid,
1253	                    revision_after=new_doc.revision,
1254	                    forward_commands=body.bundle.commands,
1255	                    undo_commands=undo_cmds,
1256	                    created_at=datetime.now(UTC),
1257	                )
1258	            )
1259	
1260	            wire_doc = document_to_wire(new_doc)
1261	            row.document = wire_doc  # type: ignore[assignment]
1262	            row.revision = new_doc.revision
1263	            await session.commit()
1264	
1265	            delta = compute_delta_wire(doc_before, new_doc)
1266	            try:
1267	                await hub.broadcast_json(
1268	                    model_id, {"type": "delta", "modelId": str(model_id), **delta}
1269	                )
1270	            except Exception:
1271	                pass
1272	
1273	    return result.model_dump(by_alias=True)

1277	# WebSocket
1278	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1279	
1280	
1281	async def websocket_loop(
1282	    websocket: WebSocket,
1283	    model_id: UUID,
1284	    hub: Hub,
1285	    resume_from: int | None = None,
1286	) -> None:
1287	
1288	    sid = str(model_id)
1289	
1290	    async with SessionMaker() as session:
1291	        row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
1292	
1293	    await websocket.accept()
1294	
1295	    if row is None:
1296	        await websocket.close(code=4404)
1297	
1298	        return
1299	
1300	    hub.subscribe(sid, websocket)
1301	
1302	    if resume_from is None:
1303	        doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
1304	        await websocket.send_json(
1305	            {
1306	                "type": "snapshot",
1307	                "modelId": sid,
1308	                "revision": doc.revision,
1309	                "elements": {
1310	                    k: el.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, el in doc.elements.items()
1311	                },
1312	                "violations": violations_wire(doc.elements),
1313	            },
1314	        )
1315	    else:
1316	        replayed = hub.resume(sid, resume_from)
1317	        if replayed is None:
1318	            await websocket.send_json({"type": "RESYNC", "modelId": sid})
1319	        else:
1320	            for payload in replayed:
1321	                await websocket.send_json(payload)
1322	            await websocket.send_json(
1323	                {
1324	                    "type": "replay_done",
1325	                    "modelId": sid,
1326	                    "resumedFrom": resume_from,
1327	                }
1328	            )
1329	
1330	    try:
1331	        while True:
1332	            msg = await websocket.receive_json()
1333	
1334	            mt = msg.get("type")
1335	
1336	            if mt == "presence_update":
1337	                pid = msg.get("peerId")
1338	
1339	                if not isinstance(pid, str) or not pid:
1340	                    continue
1341	
1342	                hub.set_peer_id(websocket, pid)
1343	
1344	                patch = {str(k): v for k, v in msg.items() if k != "type"}
1345	
1346	                hub.touch_presence(sid, pid, patch)
1347	
1348	                await hub.broadcast_presence(sid)
1349	
1350	            elif mt == "presence":
1351	                # legacy noop relay
1352	
1353	                payload = msg.get("payload", {})
1354	
1355	                await hub.broadcast_json(
1356	                    sid, {"type": "presence", "modelId": sid, "payload": payload}
1357	                )
1358	
1359	    except WebSocketDisconnect:
1360	        pass
1361	
1362	    finally:
1363	        hub.unregister(websocket)
1364

1440	    from bim_ai.tkn import decode

1456	
1457	
1458	@api_router.post("/models/{model_id}/tokens/diff")
1459	async def tokens_diff(

1461	    body: TknDiffRequest,
1462	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
1463	) -> dict[str, Any]:
1464	    """Return the structural diff between two TokenSequences."""
1465	    from bim_ai.tkn import diff
1466	    from bim_ai.tkn.types import TokenSequence
1467	
1468	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
1469	    if row is None:

1471	    seq_a = TokenSequence.model_validate(body.sequence_a)

@api_router.get("/bootstrap")
async def bootstrap(session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)) -> dict[str, Any]:
    proj_res = await session.execute(select(ProjectRecord))
    projects_out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for p in proj_res.scalars().all():
        mres = await session.execute(select(ModelRecord).where(ModelRecord.project_id == p.id))
        models = [
            {
                "id": str(m.id),
                "slug": m.slug,
                "revision": m.revision,
            }
            for m in mres.scalars().all()
        ]
        projects_out.append({"id": str(p.id), "slug": p.slug, "title": p.title, "models": models})
    return {"projects": projects_out}


class CreateEmptyModelBody(BaseModel):
    slug: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=128)
    # VIE-06: optional template id (e.g. "residential-eu") to seed the model.
    template_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="templateId")


@api_router.post("/projects/{project_id}/models")
async def create_empty_model(
    project_id: UUID,
    body: CreateEmptyModelBody,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    proj = await session.get(ProjectRecord, project_id)
    if proj is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Project not found")
    mid = uuid4()

    if body.template_id:
        if not template_exists(body.template_id):
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Template '{body.template_id}' not found")
        try:
            seed_doc = load_template_snapshot(body.template_id)
        except (FileNotFoundError, LookupError) as exc:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(exc)) from exc
        # Reset revision so the new model starts at 1 regardless of template state.
        seed_doc.revision = 1
    else:
        seed_doc = Document(revision=1, elements={})  # type: ignore[arg-type]

    # KRN-06: every new model has the singleton internal_origin from inception.
    ensure_internal_origin(seed_doc)
    ensure_sun_settings(seed_doc)
    wire = document_to_wire(seed_doc)
    row = ModelRecord(
        id=mid,
        project_id=project_id,
        slug=body.slug,
        revision=seed_doc.revision,
        document=wire,
    )
    session.add(row)
    try:
        await session.commit()
    except IntegrityError:
        await session.rollback()
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=409,
            detail="Model slug already exists for this project",
        ) from None
    return {
        "id": str(mid),
        "projectId": str(project_id),
        "slug": body.slug,
        "revision": row.revision,
        "templateId": body.template_id,
    }


@api_router.get("/templates")
async def list_template_catalog() -> dict[str, Any]:
    """VIE-06: enumerate built-in project templates."""
    rows = list_templates()
    return {
        "templates": [
            {
                "id": t.id,
                "name": t.name,
                "description": t.description,
                "thumbnailUrl": t.thumbnail_url,
            }
            for t in rows
        ]
    }
